steps.title: Identify what the question needs
steps.content: This is a Transitions question. The first sentence signals that Kramrisch and Bing both received the same award "though for different reasons." That phrase telegraphs a contrast: the second sentence must highlight the difference between their reasons. The blank transition must mark a shift or contrast between Kramrisch (honored as an art historian) and Bing (recognized as a photographer).

steps.title: Eliminate the choices
steps.content:
• Choice A — 'for instance,': "For instance" introduces an example of a previously stated idea. It does not mark a contrast, so it cannot signal the difference between the two reasons. ✗
  • Choice B — 'by contrast,': "By contrast" directly signals that what follows differs from what preceded — Bing's recognition as a photographer contrasts with Kramrisch's recognition as an art historian. This matches the "though for different reasons" signal in the first sentence. ✓
  • Choice C — 'in other words,': "In other words" restates or clarifies something already said. The second sentence is not a restatement of Kramrisch's honor; it introduces new information about Bing. ✗
  • Choice D — 'as a result,': "As a result" marks a causal relationship, implying Bing's recognition was caused by Kramrisch's. There is no causal link stated or implied. ✗
That matches choice B — B is correct.
